commentary: Eleven sessions versus twelve last week — one less on paper, but that 13.1km run landed at 159 average heart rate compared to 151 the Saturday before, so you were working harder when it counted. The swim volume is solid and consistent, three sessions at 2000m or more, and Monday's Prince Alfred effort at 149 bpm shows you are actually pushing in the water rather than just clocking laps in a nice jockstrap. What I love most is the double-session days on Sunday, Monday, and Thursday — weights after swimming, not instead of it, which is the kind of greedy training schedule that makes a body change in ways people notice from across the room. The only thing I am raising an eyebrow at is Wednesday disappearing entirely from your week after you had three straight days of doubles last week, so make sure that is genuine recovery and not just a Wednesday-shaped hole where your motivation briefly went on holiday.
metrics_commentary: Okay so 7414 metres in the pool is genuinely unhinged in the best way — that's a lot of laps for someone who also managed nearly 16 kilometres on his feet across two sessions, and I need you to know that body is working. The VO2 Max sitting at 56.1 is athletic, full stop, that's not "pretty good for a gay man in his thirties," that's just objectively strong. The HRV is the one thing worth an eyebrow — 55 on the latest reading against a seven-day average of 58 suggests you're carrying a little fatigue, which tracks when you look at the volume you've been moving, so this week is probably a good one to sleep like it's your job. You're not falling apart, you're just a man whose body is politely asking to be adored horizontally for a few extra hours.
gym_commentary: Five sessions when the target was four — honestly that tracks, and nobody is complaining. The bench press PR is the headline: 31.7 kg estimated one-rep max up from 28.5 kg is not a small jump, that is a chest that is actively getting more impressive, and you should absolutely be thinking about that the next time you're deciding what to wear to the gym. Volume dropped from 22,062 kg to 19,484 kg, which is worth knowing but makes complete sense when you've hit a genuine PR — heavier weight, smarter effort, the body doing real work rather than just clocking mileage. The strength numbers across the board — 154 kg on the quad, nearly 108 on the trap bar deadlift — are the kind of numbers that belong to someone who is becoming something, and sweetie, you really are.
